paper.title: A high-velocity star recently ejected by an intermediate-mass black hole in M15

review.summary: This paper reports the discovery of high-velocity star J0731+3717, recently ejected from the globular cluster M15 by an intermediate-mass black hole via the Hills mechanism. The paper contains one main-text parameter table comparing J0731+3717 and M15, plus two supplementary tables summarizing the top candidate globular clusters from backward orbital analysis and robustness tests under alternative assumptions. External resources include Gaia DR3, SDSS/SEGUE data archives, SDSS photometric catalogs, Dartmouth stellar isochrones, the Gala and galpy Python packages, the Baumgardt globular cluster parameter database, and a GitHub reproduction repository.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summarizes the measured astrometric, photometric, spectroscopic, kinematic, and physical parameters of the high-velocity star J0731+3717 and its proposed parent globular cluster M15 for direct comparison.

source_refs.caption: The measured parameters of J0731+3717 and M15
source_refs.label: table:Table1

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the measured physical or astrometric quantity

columns.name: J0731+3717
columns.meaning: Measured value for the high-velocity star J0731+3717

columns.name: M15
columns.meaning: Measured value for the globular cluster M15

internal_tables.evidence: The measured parameters of J0731+3717 and M15

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists the top five globular clusters sorted by the ratio of the closest orbital distance to the cluster tidal radius in the backward orbital analysis of J0731+3717.

columns.name: Closest distance (d_\rm min) | (kpc)
columns.meaning: Minimum 3D distance between the backward orbit of J0731+3717 and the cluster center

columns.name: Backward time | (Myr)
columns.meaning: Lookback time at which the closest approach occurred

columns.name: Tidal radius (r_t) | (kpc)
columns.meaning: Tidal radius of the globular cluster

columns.name: d_\rmmin/r_\rm t
columns.meaning: Ratio of closest approach distance to cluster tidal radius

internal_tables.evidence: Top 5 globular clusters sorted by the ratio of the closest orbital distance to the clusters' tidal radius

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Shows the robustness of the backward orbital reconstruction by reporting the closest orbital distance and backward time under six alternative assumptions (different Galactic potentials, solar motions, circular velocities, Galactocentric distances, moving Plummer potential for M15, and LMC potential).

columns.name: Changed assumptions
columns.meaning: Description of the modified assumption in the orbital integration test

columns.name: Closest orbital distance | (pc)
columns.meaning: Minimum 3D distance between J0731+3717 and M15 under the modified assumption

columns.name: Backward time | (Myr)
columns.meaning: Lookback time at closest approach under the modified assumption

internal_tables.evidence: Backward orbital results under different assumptions

external_resources.description: SDSS DR9 wavelength calibration documentation, referenced for the systematic offset correction applied to the SEGUE spectrum of J0731+3717.

source_refs.context: corrected for a systematic offset of $+7.3$\,km\,s$^{-1}$ due to wavelength calibrations (ref.\citep{Adelman-McCarthy08}; \url{https://www.sdss3.org/dr9/algorithms/wavelength.php}).

external_resources.evidence: corrected for a systematic offset of $+7.3$\,km\,s$^{-1}$ due to wavelength calibrations (ref.\citep{Adelman-McCarthy08}; \url{https://www.sdss3.org/dr9/algorithms/wavelength.php}).

external_resources.description: Cluster Monte Carlo (CMC) N-body simulation website, providing present-day binary semimajor axis distributions used in the Hills ejection modeling.

source_refs.context: we adopt the present-day distribution (with age up to 13.6\,Gyr; \url{https://cmc.ciera.northwestern.edu/home/}) for main-sequence binaries from the Cluster Monte Carlo (CMC) {\it N}-body simulations\citep{Kremer20}.

external_resources.evidence: we adopt the present-day distribution (with age up to 13.6\,Gyr; \url{https://cmc.ciera.northwestern.edu/home/}) for main-sequence binaries from the Cluster Monte Carlo (CMC) {\it N}-body simulations\citep{Kremer20}.

external_resources.description: Zenodo repository containing Monte Carlo N-body simulations for single-binary interactions involving compact objects (Cabrera & Rodriguez 2023), used for comparison with the Hills mechanism ejection rate.

source_refs.context: only 10 such high-velocity stars, ejected in the past 250\,Myr are found in the Monte Carlo {\it N}-body simulations (\url{https://zenodo.org/record/7599871}) for single-binary interaction involving compact objects by ref.\citep{CR23}.

external_resources.evidence: only 10 such high-velocity stars, ejected in the past 250\,Myr are found in the Monte Carlo {\it N}-body simulations (\url{https://zenodo.org/record/7599871}) for single-binary interaction involving compact objects by ref.\citep{CR23}.
